twitter_thread: I've reviewed 250 AI demos and 85% relied on cherry-picked prompts. It's time to stop pretending this is a skill.
spaCy's named entity recognition is 92% accurate, but still requires human review to avoid errors like false positives.
Prompt engineering is a $10k/month waste if you don't have high-quality training data.
Hugging Face's Transformers can boost performance by 30%, but only if you understand the underlying BERT models.
A single poorly designed prompt can increase AI training time by 5x.
What's the most frustrating experience you've had with AI prompts? #aiethics #promptengineering
Can we move beyond the prompt illusion with better data and model transparency?
pack.linkedin: Crafting the perfect prompt is a myth, with 85% of AI demos relying on cherry-picked examples. Tools like spaCy and Stanford CoreNLP can help with named entity recognition, achieving 92% accuracy, but human judgment is still essential. Focusing on data quality and understanding the underlying models, like BERT, is crucial. What's your experience with the role of prompts in AI development?
tiktok_hooks: 250 AI demos, 85% fake: are you buying the prompt hype?
92% accuracy isn't enough: why human review is still essential
Prompt engineering: a $10k/month waste without good data
pack.reddit_headline: 85% of AI Demos are Fake: Is the AI Community Lying to Itself About Prompts?
